result.checkpoint_manager:
Location: /home/polaris/wt/phase2c/src/polaris_graph/checkpoint_manager.py

Capabilities:
- get_checkpointer(): builds a langgraph AsyncSqliteSaver backed by CHECKPOINT_DB (PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR/pg_checkpoints.sqlite). This is the actual save/load engine (LangGraph persists full ResearchState per graph node).
- get_thread_id(vector_id) -> "pg_{vector_id}" (per-vector thread for independent resume).
- has_checkpoint(vector_id) -> bool (aget on the saver).
- clear_checkpoint(vector_id) -> deletes rows from checkpoints/writes tables via aiosqlite.
- A2 traceback/rewind API: list_checkpoints(vector_id, app), get_checkpoint_state(vector_id, checkpoint_id, app), rewind_to_checkpoint(vector_id, checkpoint_id, app, state_patch) plus helpers _extract_state_summary() and _serialize_state().

What it saves/loads: the entire LangGraph ResearchState snapshot (evidence, claims, sections, iteration_count, faithfulness_score, quality_metrics, final_report, status, etc.) keyed by thread_id + checkpoint_id in SQLite. Save is performed automatically by LangGraph's SqliteSaver after each node when a checkpointer is attached at compile time; the module itself only builds/queries the saver, it does not hand-roll per-step writes.

PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED: default '0' (config_defaults.py:97). PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR default 'state' (:96), PG_CHECKPOINT_LIST_LIMIT '50' (:98), PG_AUTO_RESUME '1' (:82). When PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED != '1' the whole checkpoint subsystem is skipped (graph.py:1422/1437) and all A2 functions early-return empty/error.
result.wiring_state:
PARTIALLY WIRED (native LangGraph saver) + DORMANT (A2 rewind API + the specific 2C pre-check save).

Wired: In graph.py build_and_run() (pipeline B), get_checkpointer() is called at line 1428, entered as async CM at 1498 (_optional_checkpoint), and passed to graph.compile(checkpointer=...) at 1501-1502/1530. Resume is wired at 1539-1544 (has_checkpoint -> run_input=None so LangGraph resumes). So when PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=1, LangGraph auto-saves full state after every node and can resume. This is the only place in src/ that imports checkpoint_manager (grep: only graph.py). Optional interrupt_before/interrupt_after are parsed at 1507-1528.

Dormant: (1) The A2 rewind API (list_checkpoints / get_checkpoint_state / rewind_to_checkpoint) has ZERO callers in src/. (2) There is NO purpose-built "save drafts/outline as pre-check data (never a verdict) before the faithfulness/strict_verify check" checkpoint — that Plan V4 item-2C save does not exist yet; the current mechanism only relies on LangGraph's generic per-node autosave. (3) In the honest_pipeline.py (pipeline A / benchmark) strict_verify path there is no checkpoint_manager integration at all — it is graph.py-only.
result.save_point:
Two candidate loci depending on which pipeline 2C targets:

Pipeline B (graph.py, LangGraph): drafts/outline are produced in _synthesize (defined graph.py:861; outline at 888-893, report via synthesize_report/compose_from_wiki at 901-905). The faithfulness verdict is computed upstream in _verify (~line 518-530, sets result['faithfulness_score']) and the finalize gate is _should_finalize (graph.py:1188), routed from the conditional edge at 1274-1281. The 2C pre-check save belongs AFTER _synthesize returns its drafts/outline dict but BEFORE _should_finalize reads the verdict — i.e., persist result (sections/outline/final_report) as pre-check data only, not the pass/fail verdict.

Pipeline A (honest_pipeline.py, strict_verify): the draft arrives as the draft_text parameter (line 181, already produced by the caller); the strict_verify verdict is computed at honest_pipeline.py:344 (strict = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, ...)). The exact 2C save-point is immediately BEFORE line 344 — after draft_text + evidence_pool are assembled (evidence_pool built at 343) and after Phase 3 contradiction detection (318-333), but before the strict_verify verdict. Save the draft/evidence_pool as pre-check data, never the strict_verify outcome.
result.reload_point:
Pipeline B (graph.py): reload is already wired at build_and_run lines 1537-1561 — when resume=True and a checkpoint exists (has_checkpoint), run_input is set to None (1544) so LangGraph resumes from the last saved node and re-executes forward (verify/synthesize -> _should_finalize) from scratch, i.e., the faithfulness/quality gate is re-evaluated rather than trusted from the checkpoint. This satisfies 're-verify from scratch' because only pre-check state (evidence/sections/outline) is persisted, and the verdict nodes run again on resume. The 2C reload should hook the same resume branch: on restart, load the persisted draft/outline pre-check data and re-run the faithfulness/strict_verify pass.

Pipeline A (honest_pipeline.py): there is NO reload path today. If 2C is applied here, reload must be added at the top of run_honest_pipeline (def at line 173) to detect a saved pre-check draft for the vector, load draft_text/evidence_pool, and jump back to the strict_verify pass at line 344 so the verdict is recomputed fresh (never restored from disk).
result.baseline: cd /home/polaris/wt/phase2c && timeout 200 /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q  ->  "16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 31.59s". Matches the expected 16738/11 baseline exactly. Worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c created on branch chore/review-readiness-phase2c off chore/review-readiness-phase1 (HEAD c5448ec). Oracle overlay applied: tests/oracle/cassettes/ now contains acceptance_golden.json, acceptance_llm.jsonl, acceptance_retrieval.jsonl, demo_artifact.jsonl, demo_llm.jsonl.

result.save_wiring: Save added in run_honest_pipeline immediately BEFORE the strict_verify verdict at src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py (the call `strict = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, quantified_models=...)`, originally line 344). Right after `evidence_pool = {ev["evidence_id"]: ev for ev in evidence}` is built, a flag-gated block runs: `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(): _pg2c_save_precheck(run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool)`. _pg2c_save_precheck writes ONLY {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} as JSON to PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR/pg2c_precheck_{run_id}.json — never the strict_verify verdict. Verified at runtime: saved file keys are exactly [draft_text, evidence_pool, run_id], no strict/verdict/faithfulness field. Pipeline B (graph.py) was already fully wired (get_checkpointer at 1428, compile checkpointer at 1501-1502, LangGraph autosaves full pre-check ResearchState per node, verdict nodes re-run on resume) — left untouched.
result.reload_wiring: Reload added at the TOP of run_honest_pipeline (src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py), right after `run_dir_path` is created and before the Phase 2b scope gate. Flag-gated: `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(): _pg2c_reloaded = _pg2c_load_precheck(run_id); if _pg2c_reloaded is not None: draft_text = _pg2c_reloaded.get("draft_text", draft_text)`. _pg2c_load_precheck returns only {draft_text, evidence_pool, run_id} (never a verdict). Execution then falls through normally to the Phase 4 step where strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, ...) runs FROM SCRATCH on the reloaded draft — the verdict is recomputed, never restored from disk. Three helper functions were added near the top of the module (after the logger): _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(), _pg2c_checkpoint_path(run_id), _pg2c_save_precheck(...), _pg2c_load_precheck(...). checkpoint_manager's AsyncSqliteSaver could not be reused here because pipeline A is a synchronous non-LangGraph orchestrator with no ResearchState/graph; the localized JSON helpers reuse the same resolve() accessor and PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR for consistency.
result.collection_after: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 11.61s — matches the expected 16738/11 baseline exactly. The 11 collection errors are pre-existing and unrelated to this change. Flag-OFF is byte-identical: both the save and reload call sites are wrapped entirely in `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled():`, and _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled() returns `resolve("PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED") == "1"` with default '0' (config_defaults.py:97, default unchanged). Verified at runtime: flag default -> False, flag=1 -> True, flag=0 -> False. When the flag is off no checkpoint file is written, no new branch is taken, and draft_text is unmodified — no changed ordering. py_compile passed on the changed file.

result.notes:
All four gates green on worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2c, HEAD c5448ec).

GATE 1 — FLAG-OFF byte-identical (critical): PASS. Oracle replay SHA = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 (matches required golden exactly). "replay artifact BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden" + "ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid" (exit 0). Verified BOTH with PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED unset AND explicitly =0 — identical SHA both ways. Proves normal runs are unchanged when the flag is off.

GATE 2 — Collection unchanged: PASS. "16738 tests collected, 11 errors" — matches the expected 16738/11 baseline exactly. Oracle overlay cassettes present (acceptance_golden.json, acceptance_llm.jsonl, acceptance_retrieval.jsonl, demo_artifact.jsonl, demo_llm.jsonl).

GATE 3 — Config characterization: PASS. tests/test_config_registry.py + tests/test_settings_models.py = 9 passed, 0 failed.

GATE 4 — FLAG-ON roundtrip (NEW test): PASS, 4/4. Wrote /home/polaris/wt/phase2c/tests/test_checkpoint_roundtrip.py exercising the real Plan V4 2C generation-checkpoint wiring in src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py (_pg2c_save_precheck / _pg2c_load_precheck), hermetic (no network; PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=1 and PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR set via monkeypatch to tmp_path; strict_verify stubbed with a sentinel).
  (a) checkpoint artifact IS written (pg2c_precheck_<run_id>.json) and contains the drafts/outline — the draft_text (with '# Outline'/'## Section' headers) plus the evidence_pool. PASS.
  (b) artifact contains NO faithfulness/strict_verify verdict field: persisted keys are EXACTLY {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool}; asserted absence of faithfulness/faithfulness_score/faithfulness_pct/strict/strict_verify/verdict/kept_sentences/dropped_sentences/verification/verified/passed both structurally and as raw-byte substrings. PASS.
  (c) reload re-runs verification (does not read a verdict from disk): _pg2c_load_precheck returns pre-check data only (verdict-free); a sentinel-patched strict_verify is proven to be invoked exactly once on the reloaded draft — mirroring honest_pipeline.py Phase 4 (line 453) where strict_verify runs from scratch on the restored draft after reload (line 303-306). PASS.

NO VERDICT PERSISTED — confirmed. The 2C checkpoint is deliberately pre-check-only: honest_pipeline.py saves {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} BEFORE strict_verify is computed (line 445-453) and on reload restores only draft_text (line 303-306), then recomputes strict_verify at line 453. A poisoned/partial verdict can never be trusted from disk. The design comment at lines 70-80 and 297-302 documents this invariant, and the test locks it in.

Note on scope: honest_pipeline.py is a synchronous non-LangGraph orchestrator; its 2C checkpoint (JSON, pre-check only) is separate from the heavier LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver in src/polaris_graph/checkpoint_manager.py (A2 traceback/rewind). The task's roundtrip contract (drafts/outline saved, no verdict, reload re-verifies) maps precisely to the 2C JSON path, which is what the new test targets.

codex_points: CODEX (GPT-5.6, medium effort) returned CHECKPOINT-SAFE, exit 0. Evidence independently verified against worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c HEAD c5448ec before gating.

Q1 Flag-off byte-identical: YES. Both the read (line 303) and write (line 451) call sites are fully guarded by _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(); config default PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED='0' unchanged (config_defaults.py:97); oracle SHA 9c0a3d43...db98 reproduced with the flag both unset and explicitly =0.

Q2 Pre-check data only: YES. _pg2c_save_precheck writes exactly {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} and runs BEFORE strict_verify (save at 451-452, strict_verify at 453), so the faithfulness verdict physically cannot be persisted. GATE 4 test asserts absence of every verdict-shaped key both structurally and as raw-byte substrings.

Q3 Reload re-verifies from scratch: YES. _pg2c_load_precheck restores only draft_text (line 306); strict_verify is then invoked fresh on that draft at line 453; the sentinel test confirms exactly one fresh invocation. No verdict is read from disk.

Q4 Poisoned/partial checkpoint cannot corrupt faithfulness: confirmed. Malformed JSON, missing draft_text key, and load errors all fall back to the supplied draft_text (a REQUIRED kwarg, so the .get() default is always valid); save errors are swallowed and never skip verification; stored evidence_pool is never restored so tampering is inert. A hostile draft swapped on disk can replace candidate content but must still pass fresh strict_verify and renders only from strict.kept_sentences — no verdict bypass.

Non-blocking robustness note (CODEX): _pg2c_load_precheck does not type-validate that draft_text is a str or that run_id matches; this is an availability/robustness concern only, not a path to restoring or bypassing a verdict. Does not block CHECKPOINT-SAFE.
Scope note: gate covers Pipeline A (honest_pipeline.py) synchronous JSON pre-check checkpoint only; Pipeline B LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver was left untouched.
